checks.a_analysis: I reviewed the JSON output against the source PDF to identify any hallucinated or invented text. The metadata title '911 Board Education Committee Meeting Minutes April 21 2022' contains an incorrect date, but this is an AI-generated field allowed by the instructions. All other body text and headings in the output correspond to content present in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were found.

The source document does not contain explicit contact information like URLs or email addresses. It mentions meeting attendance via 'Phone', and the output correctly reflects these 'X' markings and associated notes (e.g., join/leave times) in the attendees table. No fabricated contact details were identified.

All three pages of the source document are represented in the output. The sections transition correctly between pages (e.g., item 7 split between page 2 and 3). While content within a section is missing (see C5), the sections themselves and the pages as a whole are present.

The attendee table on page 1 and the voting tables on page 2 were cross-referenced. The attendee table in the JSON correctly aggregates rows for Committee Members (9), Guests (3), and Staff (11). The voting tables for items 4 and 5 accurately reflect the 5 members listed in the source. All tables have the correct number of data rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Agenda item 10, 'Community College –', is present as a heading in the output's list (reading_order 20, item index 2), but it is entirely missing its body content. In the source document, this section includes three sub-points (a, b, and c) totaling approximately 14 lines of text detailing partnerships, training at Cape Fear Community College, and 'Be Rail Safe' training. The output completely omits this descriptive text, losing roughly 30% of the content on page 3. This constitutes a major omission within the section.
checks.b_evidence_source:
10. Community College –
a) Partnerships – Ms. Turbeville reviewed a meeting... [paragraphs regarding Tracy McPherson, NC Community College System]
b) Cape Fear Community College Telecommunicator Training – Ms. Turbeville shared... [paragraphs regarding free NENA/APCO training]
c) Be Rail Safe Training for Telecommunicators- Ms. Turbeville welcomed guests... [paragraphs regarding Roger Smock, Ike Avery, and DOT Be Rail Safe Program]
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 3, Agenda Item 10, JSON element reading_order 20 (list item index 2)

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: I compared wording throughout the document for meaning-altering changes. The text generally preserves the source's meaning accurately. A minor OCR error exists in item 6 where 'CMRS' (Commercial Mobile Radio Service) appears as 'CRMS', but per the instructions, minor OCR differences are acceptable and do not warrant a failure.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text blocks. Each paragraph and list item from the source is represented once in its respective section.
The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. H1 is used for the organization title, H2 for the meeting name, H3 for the document status, and H2 for the 'Agenda' section. No levels are skipped.

The attendee tables on page 1 are structurally corrupted in the output. The source contains a primary attendee table (Committee Members/Guests) and a separate table for 'Staff'. The output merges these into a single table object. Because the primary table has 6 columns and the staff table has 3, the resulting merged table has rows with inconsistent cell counts, leading to structural corruption where 'Staff' headers are placed in the middle of a 6-column structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Separate tables on page 1 for 'Committee Members / Guests' and 'Staff'.
checks.b_evidence_output: A single table at element index 7 containing both sets of data, with rows starting at index 11 having only 3 cells in a 6-column table structure.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the logical flow of the source document from top to bottom, including the transition between pages.

The Agenda list (items 1-11) is significantly corrupted. The source presents a single numbered list across three pages. The output fragments this into two separate unordered lists and several individual paragraphs (for items 4-7). Additionally, the nested sub-items for item 10 (a, b, and c), which are present on page 3 of the source, are entirely omitted from the output, representing a major structural failure of the list element.
checks.b_evidence_source: A single continuous numbered list (1-11) starting on page 1 and ending on page 3, with nested items (a, b, c) under item 10.
checks.b_evidence_output: Items 1-3 are in an unordered list (reading_order 9). Items 4-7 are separate paragraphs (reading_order 12, 14, 16, 17). Items 8-11 are in a second unordered list (reading_order 20). Sub-items 10a, 10b, and 10c are missing.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any functional hyperlinks, and no links have been injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: Several significant layout discrepancies exist between the source PDF and the HTML output. First, the NC DIT 911 Board logo, which acts as the document's header in the PDF, is misplaced in the HTML, appearing between Agenda items 3 and 4. Second, the 'Committee Members' and 'Guests' lists, which are distinct side-by-side tables in the source, have been merged into a single wide table in the HTML. This layout change creates incorrect visual row-level associations between unrelated people in the two lists. Third, the sub-items (a, b, and c) for Agenda item 10 ('Community College') on page 3 of the PDF are completely missing from the HTML rendering, disrupting the document's organizational structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Logo at top-left of page 1; two separate side-by-side tables for Attendees; sub-items 10a, 10b, 10c on page 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: Logo misplaced after Agenda item 3; merged 6-column table for Attendees; sub-items 10a-c omitted under 'Community College' heading.

checks.a_analysis: Although the first table incorrectly merges two distinct source tables (a layout error noted in V1), the HTML tables themselves are visually sound. They maintain clear borders, distinct headers, and properly aligned cells. The data remains readable and structured within a table format.

The primary NC DIT 911 Board logo is present in the document. The 'Approved' watermark seen in the source PDF is absent, but this is an expected and intentional accessibility improvement as per the guidelines for decorative background elements.

No visual duplication of body content was found. The repetition of document titles and section headings in the 'Contents' navigation sidebar and the main body is an intentional accessibility feature and not a content duplication defect.
